Page Theme
Ocean Light keeps the airy blue studio look.

Form.io A-Z Interactive Playground

Choose an HTML attribute, CSS property, or CSS function from the full A-Z lists. Tweak values and copy live syntax.

Workflow Guide

Step 1: Select Item

Pick `Category`, then choose an `Item`. Use search to narrow long A-Z lists quickly.

Step 2: Tweak Parameters

Edit values for the selected item. For `HTML Attribute -> style`, use the Style Property Helper.

Step 3: Validate Syntax

Review Template vs Live Output. When it looks right, send it to the builder as reusable CSS.

Step 4: Build Stylesheet

Set class/selector/media layer, stack rules, manage variables, then export the final stylesheet.

Workspace View

Show only the controls you need right now. You can switch views at any time.

Build shows the core workflow only: choose an item, adjust it, build CSS, and preview it.

Beginner Mode

Enable this to get one numbered “do this now” instruction at a time, with a direct jump to the relevant control.

Current Step
Step 1 of 6
Completion Check
Not checked yet.

1) Select Item

This chooses the source syntax block you are editing.

Why These Are Separate
Scope
HTML attributes change the markup on the element itself.
Output Shape
Attributes produce HTML syntax like class="..." or disabled.
Best Use
Use attributes for markup hooks, semantics, and one-off inline values.
Quick Item Brief
Choose an item to inspect it.
Waiting
What It Does
Pick any item to see what it changes.
Good For
Use this box to compare options before you start changing values.
Limits
Each category has different rules around reuse, browser behavior, and scope.
Keep In Mind
After selecting an item, confirm whether it should stay inline or move into reusable CSS.
Baseline Syntax
No item selected yet.

Syntax source: same A-Z entries as your fourth file.

2) Tweak Parameters

Edit placeholders/values. Changes apply instantly to Live Output and Preview.

Pick the property that will receive the function value. Only use Custom when the preset list does not fit.

Function output is only a value like blur(6px), not a full declaration.
So this field decides the left side: property: value;.
filter: blur(6px);
Choose a property first. If it has standard named options, this will switch to a dropdown automatically.

Pick a property and value, then inject it into the style attribute textarea.

Current Selection Guide
What It Does
Choose an item to see a plain-language explanation.
How To Use It
The guide updates live as you switch between attributes, properties, and functions.
Next Step
Pick an item, adjust the values, then inspect the preview and syntax output.
No item selected yet.

Guided Recipes

Load a ready-made lesson, study the result, then use it as a starting point for your own class.

Choose a recipe to begin.

Each recipe loads a full working class into the builder and points you to the core concept behind it.

Focus Concept
The main CSS idea this recipe is teaching.
Preview Target
The preview element this recipe will style.

    Contextual Glossary

    Search a concept, read it in plain language, then jump to the matching control in the app.

    Definition
    Choose a term to see a plain-language explanation.
    Where You Use It
    The related controls will be identified here.
    Example
    Examples appear here.
    Practical Tip
    Tips help you avoid common mistakes.

    3) Syntax

    Template is the source pattern. Live Output is the resolved syntax from your current values.

    
          
    
          
    
            
            
    
          
    
          
    
            
          
    
        
    

        
    
          
    6) Visual Diff Inspector
    
          
    Compares the active preview target before vs after styling and points to the declarations most likely causing the change.
    
          
    
            
    
              
    Active Preview Target
    
              
    Card
    
            
    
            
    
              
    Detected Visual Changes

    7) Advanced Selector Simulator
    
          
    Use this only when you need selectors that depend on structure, such as child, sibling, or nested rules.
    
          
    
            
    What it does: It renders a small editable HTML tree and applies the CSS already stacked in the CSS Builder.
    
            
    Important: Step 2 draft tweaks do not appear here until you add them to the builder. This panel is for testing selector logic, not one-off declarations.
    
            
    Quick test: Load a template, keep auto-apply on, then add a builder rule that targets a child or sibling such as .my-custom-class .sim-field + .sim-help.

    4) CSS Builder
    
          
    Use this as your stylesheet workspace: selector targeting, media layers, variables, rule stack, and presets.
    
          
    
            
            
            
              
              
              
              
              
              
              
              
              
              
              
              
              
              
              
              
              
              
              
              
              
              
            
          
    
          
    
            
            
    
              
              
            
    
            
    Use this when you want injected CSS to target Form.io's built-in selectors directly instead of a custom wrapper class.
    
          
    
          
    
            
            
          
    
          
    
            
    
              
              
            
    
            
    
              
              
            
    
          
    
          
    
            
    
              
              
              
              
    Use Hover for mouse-over styles, Focus for clicked/tabbed fields, and Active for pressed/clicked states.

    Guided composer is active.

Use the guided selector controls until you need manual syntax.
